content:
I also really like your interpretation of the scarecrow as a symbol of fear. Phoenix’s initial reaction and subsequent laugh highlight her capacity to face adversity with humor and determination. When Phoenix first encounters the scarecrow, she remarks, “That old scarecrow... it’s a shame to scare children like that” (Welty p. 7). Her laughter after realizing it’s just a scarecrow shows her ability to not let her fears control her and emphasizes her resilience. It seems that Welty is showing us that even when we are confronted with something that frightens us, the strength to continue lies in how we perceive and react to it. Phoenix's journey, then, is not only one of endurance, but of a deeper inner strength that comes from her love and sense of duty to her family.

Your point about love being the driving force behind Phoenix’s perseverance is spot on. Her journey is about more than just physical hardship. It’s about the emotional and spiritual dedication she has to her grandson, and how that love can fuel someone through the toughest of circumstances. As Phoenix herself reflects, "I will go on, and I will go on" (Welty p. 8). This determination is rooted in her commitment to her grandson, demonstrating that love truly is her guiding force.
